cards.title: Parents: Tell us how you're balancing work and childcare this summer
cards.body: The summer holidays bring annual challenges for parents juggling work with childcare responsibilities. With rising holiday childcare costs, The Guardian wants to hear how you're managing this season.

cards.title: I was struggling after eight rounds of IVF – then a midwife became my surrogate
cards.body: Narzny McKinney and her surrogate Sarah discuss their surrogacy experience and address common misconceptions about the practice. Their story highlights the emotional and practical realities of building a family through surrogacy.

cards.title: UK surrogacy law 'creaking at seams' as demand rises sharply
cards.body: Experts are urging ministers to update surrogacy legislation to prevent intended parents from resorting to riskier arrangements abroad. The current legal framework is struggling to keep pace with rising demand.

cards.title: Listening to the Father Within
cards.body: Regret becomes transformative when fathers replace condemnation with curiosity about parenting mistakes. Through exploring hidden patterns beneath errors, healing and growth can begin.

cards.title: Why You Can Be Proud of Yourself After Psychosis
cards.body: Recovery from psychosis involves navigating crushing emotions while rebuilding self-identity. Taking pride in the recovery process itself, rather than just the outcome, supports lasting wellness.

cards.title: Why Calling Your Child Picky May Matter More Than You Think
cards.body: Labeling children as "picky eaters" may actually reinforce picky eating behaviors. Small language shifts in how parents discuss food can help kids become more confident eaters.

cards.title: There Is No "Attention Deficit" in Attention-Deficit Disorder
cards.body: Children with ADHD can hyperfocus on engaging activities like video games while struggling with less interesting tasks like homework. Understanding this distinction helps parents and educators support better.

cards.title: Are the Kids Alright? Teens' Usage of AI Companions Is Up
cards.body: Teenagers are increasingly turning to artificial intelligence for companionship, support, and emotional connection. Understanding why young people seek AI relationships and the potential risks is crucial for parents.
